raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Much of the crude oil imported into California comes from foreign nations with demonstrated human rights abuses or lower environmental standards than California.",
    "The Geologic Energy Management Division (CalGem) will report the amount of particulate matter released into the air from tanker ship emissions due to imported oil.",
    "In 2020, California was the seventh largest oil producing state in the nation with an average production of 391,000 barrels per day.",
    "California's demand for oil is 1.8 million barrels per day, leading to a dependency on imported oil.",
    "California consumers may send $50 billion a year to other countries if oil prices remain around $100 a barrel.",
    "The top two countries from which California imports oil are Saudi Arabia and Ecuador.",
    "Petroleum is used for more than just gasoline; over 6,000 everyday products are made from petroleum.",
    "The oil industry in California directly supports 55,000 jobs with an average salary of $123,000 per year.",
    "The average salary in California is $63,783 per year, approximately half of the average salary in the oil industry."
  ]
}
```
